spec_match.rationale: The mDL example uses `protocol: 'preview'` — which the sibling protocol-explorer page correctly flags as DEPRECATED and removed in v143. The mDL issuance flow should use `org.iso.mdoc` (or be carried inside an openid4vci request). Showing 'preview' as the mDL example contradicts the protocol-explorer's own deprecation notice and would error in Chrome 143.

interactive.rationale: Tab switcher between mDL and VC flows, live request body preview, 'Try it' button that invokes navigator.credentials.create. 3 controls.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'two real deployed issuance shapes side-by-side: ISO mobile driving licence vs W3C Verifiable Credentials over OpenID4VCI. Same API, very different payloads.' Page delivers this contrast.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles missing navigator.credentials.create; expected error path well-explained. Doesn't show what happens when the wallet returns vs when there's no wallet. Doesn't probe whether the chrome://flags is on.

openQuestions.title: Replace `protocol: 'preview'` for mDL with `org.iso.mdoc` (or carry inside openid4vci)
openQuestions.detail: The sibling protocol-explorer page lists `preview` as DEPRECATED and 'Removed in v143'. Yet this concept uses `protocol: 'preview'` for the mDL example. The correct shape per chromestatus is either `protocol: 'org.iso.mdoc'` with itemsRequest, OR `protocol: 'openid4vci'` carrying mso_mdoc format inside DCQL. Right now the page actively teaches the deprecated protocol.

openQuestions.title: Add the flag-enable / origin-trial banner
openQuestions.detail: Same as the credential-issuance sibling. Without it, the 'Try it' button always errors and the demo looks broken. CLAUDE.md rule #7 requires the banner.

openQuestions.title: Annotate which steps happen in JS vs in Chrome vs in the wallet
openQuestions.detail: The 5-step list mixes UI prompt, JS call, Chrome dispatch, wallet handshake, JS response. Colour-coding (or icons) for which actor performs each step would help visitors understand which layer to debug when things go wrong.

openQuestions.title: Show the response shape side-by-side too
openQuestions.detail: Request bodies are shown, but the response payloads (mDL ciphertext bytes vs VC JWT) are described in prose only. Adding sample response objects for each flow would close the loop visually.

summary: Useful side-by-side framing of the two deployment shapes — but the mDL example uses `protocol: 'preview'`, which the sibling protocol-explorer correctly flags as removed in v143. That's a hard spec_match bug. Fix it to use `org.iso.mdoc` or openid4vci-carrying-mso_mdoc, and add the flag banner.
